title: Operation First Contact
learning_objectives: Identify and exploit initial access vectors
Document attacker methodologies from threat intelligence
Establish initial foothold with minimal detection
Create persistence mechanisms for follow-up operations
key_topics: Red team engagement planning and scoping
scenario: Scenario MineralTech Corp in Botswana has mature email filtering, EDR, and security-aware employees. Standard phishing will fail. Design and simulate advanced initial access. Subtasks Scoring Rubric Linked Recipes: RA02, RA03 ________________________________________________________________________________ 3.2 Advanced Exploitation

scenario_context.setting: Johannesburg, South Africa, East African telecommunications provider
scenario_context.challenge: Identify and exploit initial access vector against East African telecommunications provider in Johannesburg, South Africa
scenario_context.relevance: Reflects real-world African enterprise security challenges and red team operations across the continent

subtasks.title: Target Reconnaissance
subtasks.description: Deep OSINT on employees. Identify high-value targets.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Begin by thoroughly reviewing the scenario and understanding all objectives. Document your approach before execution. This is the foundational subtask that enables all following work.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Pretext Development
subtasks.description: 3 distinct pretext scenarios that bypass phishing awareness.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Conduct systematic reconnaissance and information gathering. Map the target network, identify services, and document all findings with evidence.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Payload Engineering
subtasks.description: Payloads evading email gateways: HTML smuggling, macro-free.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Analyze vulnerabilities discovered in reconnaissance. Validate each vulnerability through testing and document the exploitation path.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Infrastructure Setup
subtasks.description: Phishing infrastructure: domains, landing pages, redirectors.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Execute the exploitation with careful attention to OPSEC and evasion. Maintain detailed logs of all actions for evidence and reporting.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Campaign Simulation
subtasks.description: Complete campaign plan with timing and decision trees.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Establish persistence mechanisms that survive system reboots and will maintain access throughout the engagement.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Detection Analysis
subtasks.description: What artifacts remain? What could blue team detect?
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Perform privilege escalation and lateral movement to achieve the mission objectives. Document each pivot point.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

resources: OCH Advanced Red Team Operations Toolkit

quiz.question: What is the typical first step in a red team engagement's attack chain?
quiz.explanation: Initial access requires reconnaissance to identify vulnerabilities and attack vectors.

quiz.question: Which threat intelligence source would be most valuable for identifying zero-day vulnerabilities in a target organization?
options: Commercial threat feeds including dark web monitoring
quiz.explanation: Commercial threat feeds with dark web monitoring provide early warning of emerging exploits.

mentor_notes: For OA-M02 - Operation First Contact: Ensure learner demonstrates proper OPSEC, evidence collection, and professional reporting. This mission is designed to build red team operations expertise. Coordinate with blue team for detection validation where applicable.
